directive.title: Mailgun email notification on RFQ status change

auditor.summary: The build implements the core Mailgun email notification functionality with proper fire-and-forget pattern, audit logging for failures, and configuration validation. However, it has a CRITICAL issue: unauthorized modification of lib/db/schema.ts which is outside the build specification's scope and introduces schema changes that may break existing code. The TypeScript compilation cannot be guaranteed to succeed due to these schema changes and potential import path issues. Additionally, several acceptance criteria lack corresponding integration tests. The build is CONDITIONAL - it can ship after reverting the unauthorized schema changes, fixing import paths, and adding the missing integration tests.

top_issues.description: Modified existing schema file that was not in the build specification's files_to_modify list. The build spec only lists lib/notifications/email.ts, app/api/rfq/[id]/status/route.ts, and .env.example as files to create/modify. Modifying lib/db/schema.ts without authorization could break other parts of the system that depend on the original schema.

The actor_name field added to auditLogs table does not exist in the ground truth schema. This will cause TypeScript compilation errors in any existing code that imports auditLogs without the actor_name field.

Import path '@/lib/db' may not resolve correctly. The ground truth shows db/index.ts exists, but the import pattern '@/lib/db' could be ambiguous if there's also a lib/db.ts file. This is a BUILDABILITY violation.
